exercises.title: Mock default returns
exercises.scenario: Unstubbed mocks return default values — null for objects, 0 for ints.

exercises.title: Stub with when().thenReturn()
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blanks to stub a UserRepository.

blanks.hint: Mockito factory method to create a mock

exercises.title: @Mock and @InjectMocks setup
exercises.scenario: Annotations simplify mock creation and injection.
exercises.instructions: Write a test class for `GreetingService` which depends on `UserRepository`. Use @Mock, @InjectMocks, @ExtendWith. Stub findById to return 'Aisha'. Test that greet('U01') returns 'Hello, Aisha!'.
hints: @Mock for each dependency, @InjectMocks for the class under test

exercises.title: verify() — check method was called
exercises.scenario: verify() asserts a mock method was invoked.
exercises.instructions:
Test that `OrderService.processOrder()` calls `repo.save()` exactly once and `emailSvc.send()` exactly once.

Write the test with both verify statements.
hints: No when() needed for void methods unless you need to stub them

exercises.title: thenThrow — stub exceptions
exercises.scenario: Mock can throw exceptions to test error paths.
exercises.instructions:
Test that `UserService.deleteUser(id)` throws `RuntimeException` when `repo.deleteById(id)` throws it. Also verify that no notification is sent when deletion fails.

Write two tests: one for successful delete, one for failed.
hints: Test the error path: repo failure must prevent notification

exercises.title: verify(times(n)) and verify(never())
exercises.scenario: Control how many times a method should be called.
exercises.instructions:
Test `ReportService.generateBatch(List<String> ids)` which calls `writer.write()` once per ID and `emailSvc.sendSummary()` once at the end.

For 3 IDs: verify write called 3 times, summary called 1 time.
hints: Empty list: writer never called, summary sent with count=0

exercises.title: ArgumentCaptor — verify saved object
exercises.scenario: Capture the argument passed to a mock to assert its fields.
exercises.instructions:
Test that `ProductService.createProduct(String name, double price)` saves a Product with correct name, price, and createdAt timestamp (not null).

Use ArgumentCaptor to capture the saved Product.

exercises.title: any() and eq() argument matchers
exercises.scenario: Matchers let stubs respond to flexible or specific arguments.
exercises.instructions:
Test `SearchService.search(String query, int limit)` with argument matchers:
- For any query with limit=10, return a 10-item list
- For query='mouse', any limit, return a 2-item list
- For null query, throw IllegalArgumentException

Verify each behaviour.
hints: Must use eq() when mixing with anyString()/anyInt()
eq(10) wraps a literal for use with matchers
null query check happens before repo — verifyNoInteractions for strict test

exercises.title: verifyNoInteractions — strict isolation
exercises.scenario: verifyNoInteractions fails if unexpected calls happened.
exercises.instructions:
Test `PaymentService.refund(String paymentId, boolean notify)` where:
- notify=true: calls repo.refund() then emailSvc.sendRefundEmail()
- notify=false: calls repo.refund() but NOT emailSvc

Use verifyNoInteractions for the notify=false test.
hints: verifyNoInteractions(emailSvc) — nothing was called on it
Stricter than verify(emailSvc, never()) — checks ALL methods

exercises.title: Mock vs Spy — default behaviour
exercises.scenario: Mock returns null/default; Spy calls real methods.
hints: spy.add() calls the real ArrayList.add() — size becomes 1

exercises.title: InOrder — verify call sequence
exercises.scenario: InOrder ensures methods were called in the right order.
exercises.instructions:
Test that `TransactionService.transfer(from, to, amount)` calls:
1. First: repo.debit(from, amount)
2. Then: repo.credit(to, amount)
3. Then: auditLog.record(from, to, amount)

Use InOrder to verify the sequence.
hints: InOrder inOrder = inOrder(repo, audit) — pass all mocks involved
Order matters: debit before credit, credit before audit

exercises.title: Complete user registration service test
exercises.scenario: Full service test with multiple mocks and verifications.
exercises.instructions:
Write 4 tests for `UserRegistrationService.register(String email, String password)`:
- Success: user saved, welcome email sent, returns userId
- Duplicate email: repo.exists() returns true → throws DuplicateUserException, no save
- Invalid email: throws IllegalArgumentException before repo check
- Password too short: throws IllegalArgumentException before repo check
hints: Duplicate: exists returns true → throw before save
Invalid inputs: no repo interaction at all
eq() needed when mixing with anyString() in save stub

exercises.title: Spy — partially mock a real object
exercises.scenario: Spy calls real methods but can override specific ones.
exercises.instructions:
Use Mockito.spy() to test `PriceCalculator.totalWithTax(List<Item> items)` where:
- sumItems() is the real method (tested indirectly)
- getTaxRate() is stubbed (different environment rates)

Test two scenarios: standard 18% tax and reduced 5% tax.
hints: Mockito.spy(new PriceCalculator()) — real object, can stub methods
doReturn(0.18).when(spy).getTaxRate() — use doReturn for spy, not when()
sumItems() remains real — calls actual stream sum

exercises.title: Captor for complex objects — verify audit trail
exercises.scenario: Capture and verify complex objects passed to mocks.
exercises.instructions: Test `PaymentService.processPayment(Payment p)` which creates an `AuditEntry` and passes it to `auditRepo.record()`. Capture the AuditEntry and verify its fields: paymentId, amount, status='PROCESSED', timestamp (not null).

exercises.title: Complete Razorpay-style payment service test
exercises.scenario: Full mock test suite for a payment processing service.
exercises.instructions:
Write a comprehensive test suite (6+ tests) for `RazorpayPaymentService.charge(String userId, double amount)`:
- Success: user found, funds sufficient, charge processed, receipt created, notification sent
- User not found: repo throws EntityNotFoundException, nothing else happens
- Insufficient funds: balanceService returns insufficient, no charge, no receipt
- Charge fails: paymentGateway throws, no receipt, notification sent with FAILED status
- Zero amount: IllegalArgumentException before any service call
- Verify exact amount passed to gateway using ArgumentCaptor
hints: Helper setUp method for happy path stubs — less repetition
Gateway fails: receipt never created, but FAILED notification IS sent
User not found: balance/gateway/receipt/notify all have NO interactions
